|-
|style="text-align: left;"| '''Sports Psychologist''' || {{flagicon|DEN}} Carsten Hvid
|}

===Managers===
{{main|Denmark national football team statistics#Managers}}
The responsibility and job description for the manager of the Denmark national football team has changed throughout the history. From 1908 to 1918, the DBU preferred to assign a manager with the overall responsibility. This was followed by a long era from 1919 to 1956 where either no manager or only a [[caretaker manager]] was assigned. When the DBU assigned [[Arne Sørensen (Danish footballer)|Arne Sørensen]] (November 1956&nbsp;– December 1961) and [[Poul Petersen (footballer)|Poul Petersen]] (January 1962&nbsp;–December 1966) as managers for a rolling period of several years, they were given the responsibility to coach the physical training and decide the team tactics before and during each match, but had no influence upon the selection of players for the national squad, which was a job for the specially-appointed selection committee, the ''UdtagelsesKomitéen''.<ref name="Manager history">{{cite web|title=DBUs landstrænere i perioden 1967–69|url=http://diwa.sbu.dbu.dk/page.aspx?id=1183|author=SBU|date=26 November 2001|language=da|access-date=31 October 2010|archive-date=6 December 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181206005300/http://diwa.sbu.dbu.dk/page.aspx?id=1183|url-status=dead}}</ref>

From 1967 to 1969, the management of the team more or less became a sole responsibility for the ''UdtagelsesKomitéen'', following a new approach. In this new approach, the job to select players continued to be the responsibility for the ''UdtagelsesKomitéen'', but the chairman of the committee was now also appointed to be the "tactical manager" of the national team, as the DBU realized the need of having a special tactical manager assigned to get influence upon both the selection of players and the related tactics before and during the game. However, the appointed tactical manager did not have the responsibility to train the squad, as a special physical coach was instead selected for that job. In 1970, the approach with two managers assigned was again abandoned when the DBU realized the need to assign only one manager with the sole responsibility for the squad.<ref name="Manager history"/>

From 1970 to 1979, the job to select players was not yet a sole responsibility of the manager alone, but only a shared responsibility, as this continued to be a matter for the ''UdtagelsesKomitéen'' to decide. When a selection of players had to be made, it was decided by a vote in the committee, with the appointed manager being granted an influence of three votes and the four members of the committee being granted one vote each. This special selection procedure continued until July 1979, where it was decided to award the sole responsibility of all manager-related areas to the manager himself, with the assignment of [[Sepp Piontek]] as the first full-time professional manager of the national team.<ref name="Manager history"/>

==== Manager history ====
This is a complete overview of every national team manager since the first official game in October 1908. From 1911 to 1961, a total of 169 games were played without a designated manager.

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center"
|-

| align=left | {{flagicon|DEN}} [[Ebbe Sand]] ([[caretaker manager|caretaker]]){{efn|[[Ebbe Sand]] was caretaker on 11 November 2020, while Hjulmand was unable to perform duties. Sand was given credit for the match result.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ebbe Sand |url=https://www.dbu.dk/landshold/landsholdsdatabasen/CoachInfo/4197 |accessdate=11 October 2025 |publisher=Danish Football Association |lang=da}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=11 November 2020 |title=Vikartræner Ebbe Sand: Landskamp er berettiget trods afbud |trans-title=Caretaker coach Ebbe Sand: International match is justified despite withdrawals |url=https://jyllands-posten.dk/sport/fodbold/ECE12551977/vikartraener-ebbe-sand-landskamp-er-berettiget-trods-afbud/ |access-date=11 October 2025 |newspaper=Jyllands-Posten |language=danish |agency=Ritzau}}</ref>}}

{{Notelist}}

==== Manager records ====

* Longest reign: [[Morten Olsen]], 15 years
* Most wins: [[Morten Olsen]], 80

==Players==
{{Main|List of Denmark international footballers}}

===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] matches against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] and [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]] on 3 and 7 June 2026.<ref>{{cite news |title=Truppen til junis testkampe er udtaget |url=https://www.dbu.dk/nyheder/2026/maj/truppen-til-junis-testkampe-er-udtaget/ |access-date=27 May 2026 |publisher=Dansk Boldspil-Union |date=22 May 2026 |language=Danish}}</ref>
<br>''Caps and goals correct as of 7 June 2026, following the match against [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]].''<ref>{{cite web |title=Herrelandsholdet |publisher=DBU |url=https://www.dbu.dk/landshold/herrelandshold/a-landsholdet/ |language=da |access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref>

{{nat fs g start}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players were also called up within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>{{small|INJ}}</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>{{small|MED}}</sup> = Withdrew for non-injury related medical reasons
*<sup>{{small|PRE}}</sup> = Preliminary squad
*<sup>{{small|ST}}</sup> = Standby
*<sup>{{small|SUS}}</sup> = Serving suspension
*<sup>{{small|RET}}</sup> = Retired from national team
{{nat fs end}}

{{noteslist}}

===Player history===
The players are freely chosen by the national team manager. They are normally assembled from their respective club teams at the Hotel Marienlyst in [[Elsinore]] for a week-long training camp preluding the upcoming match. Friendly matches are typically played on a Wednesday evening. World Cup qualifiers are played on both Saturday and Wednesday evenings, while Euro qualifiers now take place on both a Friday and Tuesday evening when playing two matches in a row, or on a Friday/Saturday/Tuesday at rounds with only one match scheduled.<ref name=Program>{{cite web|title=Landsholdets program|url=http://www.dbu.dk/sitecore/content/Faelles/landshold/herrelandshold/A-landsholdstruppen/landsholdets_program.aspx|author=DBU|date=20 May 2010|language=da|access-date=29 October 2010|archive-date=19 November 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101119081519/http://www.dbu.dk/sitecore/content/Faelles/landshold/herrelandshold/A-landsholdstruppen/landsholdets_program.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Danmark mod Island først og Portugal sidst i EM-kvalifikationen |url=http://www.dbu.dk/Nyheder/2010/Marts/danmark_mod_island_foerst_og_portugal_sidst_i_em-kvalifikationen.aspx |author=DBU|date=25 March 2010|language=da}}</ref>

===Best players of all time===
In November 2006, the DBU nominated eight Denmark national team footballers for the "Best Danish Footballer of All Time" award. The nominated players were [[Morten Olsen]], [[Henning Jensen]], [[Allan Simonsen]], [[Preben Elkjær]], [[Michael Laudrup]], [[Brian Laudrup]], [[Peter Schmeichel]] and [[Jon Dahl Tomasson]]. The winner of the award was decided by a public vote arranged by the broadcasting channel [[TV 2 (Denmark)|TV2]], which ended with Michael Laudrup as a clear winner, with 58% of the votes.<ref>{{cite web |title=Michael Laudrup -Danmarks bedste gennem tiderne |url=http://www.bt.dk/sport/michael-laudrup-danmarks-bedste-gennem-tiderne |author=BT.dk|date=13 November 2006|language=da}}</ref>

===Hall of Fame===
{{main|Danish Football Hall of Fame}}
Denmark's Hall of Fame was established in October 2008, as a special award to celebrate the best footballers, teams and coaches; throughout the history of the national team. A jury with six people (representing the newspapers, authors of football books, active players of the national team, [[TV 2 (Denmark)|TV2]], the DBU, and a Hall of Fame member), each year has the job to award one or two new members for the Hall of Fame. The award is handed out at the official Danish Football Awards, which is a yearly television broadcast event&nbsp;– organized by the DBU in November throughout 2008 to 2013 and subsequently moved to a later date in February. Besides receiving the award, all Hall of Fame members are also invited to leave a footprint in bronze, to decorate the entrance hall at "Fodboldens hus" -the DBU headquarters in [[Brøndby]].<ref name="HoF-1">{{cite web|title=Fodbolden får egen Hall of Fame|url=http://www.dbu.dk/news/newsShow.aspx?id=248749|author=DBU|date=1 October 2008|language=da|access-date=10 May 2009|archive-date=6 March 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120306025719/http://www.dbu.dk/news/newsShow.aspx?id=248749|url-status=dead}}</ref> When the DBU celebrated its 125-year anniversary in May 2014, it decided to make an extraordinary award of nine additional Hall of Fame Members, all playing during the first half of the DBU national team's existence, from 1908 to 1964.<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014">{{cite news|url=http://politiken.dk/sport/fodbold/ECE2292460/ni-nye-danskere-er-optaget-i-fodboldens-hall-of-fame/|title=Ni nye danskere er optaget i fodboldens Hall of Fame|language=da|newspaper=Politiken|date=17 May 2014}}</ref> Because of financial restraints, the DBU cancelled the planned televised broadcast of the Football Award in February 2015, and for the same reason opted not to award any new Hall of Fame members in this specific year. The DBU expected to return awarding new Hall of Fame members again, when the next broadcast Football Award event was organized in February 2016.<ref name="No HoF-members awarded in 2015">{{cite web|url=http://www.dbu.dk/Nyheder/2015/Februar/DFA_2014|title=Hvem er Danmarks bedste i 2014?|publisher=DBU|date=4 February 2015|access-date=4 May 2015|archive-date=2 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170202161425/http://www.dbu.dk/Nyheder/2015/Februar/DFA_2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

====Hall of Fame members====
Sorted by year of last appearance with the national team.
{{div col|colwidth=30em}}
#'''[[Sophus Nielsen|Sophus "Krølben" Nielsen]]''' (1908–1919)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Nils Middelboe]]''' (1908–1920)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Sophus Hansen|Sophus "Målmand" Hansen]]''' (1911–1920)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Carl Hansen (footballer)|Carl "Skoma'r" Hansen]]''' (1918–1921)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Poul Nielsen|Poul "Tist" Nielsen]]''' (1910–1925)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Michael Rohde (footballer)|Michael Rohde]]''' (1915–1931)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/michael-rohde/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Michael Rohde|work=DBU|date=2023|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Valdemar Laursen]]''' (1918–1934)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/valdemar-laursen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Valdemar Laursen |work=DBU|date=2023|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Fritz Tarp]]''' (1918–1934)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/fritz-tarp/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Fritz Tarp|work=DBU|date=2023|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Pauli Jørgensen]]''' (1925–1939)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Football at the 1948 Summer Olympics – Men's team squads#Denmark|1948 Summer Olympics team]]''' (July–August 1948)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/herrernes-ol-hold-fra-1948/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Herrernes OL-hold fra 1948|work=DBU|date=2014|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[John Hansen (footballer, born 1924)|John Hansen]]''' (1948)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/john-hansen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – John Hansen |work=DBU|date=21 March 2016|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Carl Aage Præst]]''' (1945–1949)<ref name="HoF-2">{{cite web|title=To nye medlemmer af Hall of Fame|url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2008-11-17-to-nye-medlemmer-af-hall-of-fame|author=((TV2 Sport))|date=17 November 2008|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Jørgen Leschly Sørensen]]''' (1946–1949)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/joergen-leschly-soerensen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Jørgen Leschly Sørensen |work=DBU|date=2017|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Eigil Nielsen (footballer born 1918)|Eigil Nielsen]]''' (1940–1951)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Knud Lundberg]]''' (1943–1956)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Aage Rou Jensen]]''' (1945–1957)<ref name="TV2 Østjylland">{{cite web|url=https://www.tv2ostjylland.dk/artikel/rou-og-optaget-i-hall-fame/|title=Rou og From optaget i Hall of Fame|work=TV2 Østjylland|date=21 March 2016|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics – Men's team squads#Denmark|1960 Summer Olympics team]]''' (August–September 1960)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/herrernes-ol-hold-fra-1960/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Herrernes OL-hold fra 1960|work=DBU|date=2014|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Harald Nielsen]]''' (1959–1960)<ref name="HOF-5">{{cite web|ref=none|title=Guld-Harald kom i Hall of Fame|url=http://sporten.tv2.dk/fodbold/article.php/id-35014251:guldharald-kom-i-hall-of-fame.html|author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=15 November 2010|language=da|access-date=16 November 2010|archive-date=20 November 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101120055044/http://sporten.tv2.dk/fodbold/article.php/id-35014251:guldharald-kom-i-hall-of-fame.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>
#'''[[Henry From]]''' (1957–1961)<ref name="TV2 Østjylland"/>
#'''[[Jørgen Olesen]]''' (1951–1962)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/joergen-olesen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Jørgen Olesen |work=DBU|date=2022|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Poul Pedersen]]''' (1953–1964)<ref name="9 extra HoF-members awarded in 2014"/>
#'''[[Henning Enoksen]]''' (1958–1966)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tv2ostjylland.dk/artikel/afdoed-agf-koryfae-optaget-i-fodboldens-fineste-selskab/|title=Afdød AGF-koryfæ optaget i fodboldens fineste selskab|work=TV2 Østjylland|date=21 March 2017|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Ole Madsen]]''' (1958–1969)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/ole-madsen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Ole Madsen|work=DBU|date=21 March 2016|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Ulrik le Fevre]]''' (1965–1976)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tvsyd.dk/vejle/vejle-spiller-i-hall-of-fame-4|title=Vejle-spiller i Hall of Fame|work=TV Syd|date=26 September 2022|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Johnny Hansen (footballer, born 1943)|Johnny Hansen]]''' (1965–1978)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/johnny-hansen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Johnny Hansen|work=DBU|date=2019|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Henning Jensen]]''' (1972–1980)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dbu.dk/om-dbu/fodboldpriser/fodboldens-hall-of-fame/henning-jensen/|title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame – Henning Jensen|work=DBU|date=15 December 2017|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Per Røntved]]''' (1970–1982)<ref name="HoF-8">{{cite web|title=Hall of Fame har fået nyt medlem|url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2011-11-07-hall-of-fame-har-faaet-nyt-medlem|publisher=TV2 Sport |author=
Lars Bruun-Mortensen |date=7 November 2011|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Allan Simonsen]]''' (1972–1986)<ref name="HoF-2"/>
#'''[[Frank Arnesen]]''' (1977–1987)<ref name="HoF-10">{{cite web |title=To 80'er-ikoner optaget i Hall of Fame|url=http://sporten.tv2.dk/2013-11-11-80%E2%80%99er-ikoner-optaget-i-hall-fame |author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=11 November 2013|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Preben Elkjær]]''' (1977–1988)<ref name="HOF-6">{{cite web|ref=none|title=Preben Elkjær er i Hall of Fame|url=http://sporten.tv2.dk/article.php/id-35014178%3Apreben-elkj%C3%A6r-er-i-hall-of-fame.html?forside|author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=15 November 2010|language=da|access-date=16 November 2010|archive-date=19 March 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120319023004/http://sporten.tv2.dk/article.php/id-35014178:preben-elkj%C3%A6r-er-i-hall-of-fame.html?forside|url-status=dead}}</ref>
#'''[[Søren Lerby]]''' (1978–1989)<ref name="HoF-10"/>
#'''[[1986 FIFA World Cup squads#Denmark|Danish Dynamite team]]''' (1980–1989){{refn|group=note|Represented with a footprint of [[Morten Olsen]] ([[Captain (association football)|c]], 1970–1989) and [[Sepp Piontek]] (Manager 1979–1990).<ref name="HoF-3">{{cite web|title=Dynamit-holdet i Hall of Fame|url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2009-09-29-dynamitholdet-i-hall-of-fame| publisher=TV2 Sport |author=Lars Mortensen|date=29 September 2009|language=da}}</ref>}}
#'''[[Sepp Piontek]]''' (Manager 1979–1990)<ref name="HoF-7">{{cite web|title=Piontek optaget i Hall of Fame|url=http://sporten.tv2.dk/fodbold/article.php/id-45374561:piontek-optaget-i-hall-of-fame.html|author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=7 November 2011|language=da|access-date=2 February 2012|archive-date=12 November 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111112085112/http://sporten.tv2.dk/fodbold/article.php/id-45374561:piontek-optaget-i-hall-of-fame.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>
#'''[[Jan Mølby]]''' (1982–1990)<ref name="HoF-Molby">{{cite web |title= Mølby optaget i Hall of Fame |url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2023-06-16-moelby-optaget-i-hall-of-fame|author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=16 June 2023|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[UEFA Euro 1992 squads#Denmark|Euro 1992 team]]''' (June 1992){{refn|group=note|Represented with a footprint of [[Lars Olsen]] ([[Captain (association football)|c]], 1986–1996) and [[Richard Møller Nielsen]] (Manager 1990–1996).<ref name="HoF-1"/>}}
#'''[[Lars Høgh]]''' (1983–1995)<ref>Roth, C. (9 December 2021). [https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2021-12-08-lars-hoegh-er-doed Lars Høgh er død]. TV2.</ref>
#'''[[Richard Møller Nielsen]]''' (Manager 1990–1996)<ref name="HoF-11">{{cite web|title=Richard Møller Nielsen i fodboldens Hall of Fame|url=http://www.dbu.dk/Nyheder/2014/Februar/RMN_FHOF|author=DBU|date=1 March 2014|language=da|access-date=12 May 2014|archive-date=24 December 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161224230753/http://www.dbu.dk/nyheder/2014/februar/rmn_fhof|url-status=dead}}</ref>
#'''[[Michael Laudrup]]''' (1982–1998)<ref name="HoF-1"/>
#'''[[Brian Laudrup]]''' (1987–1998)<ref name="HoF-9">{{cite web |title=Optaget i Hall of Fame: "Den bedste af dem alle" |url=http://sporten.tv2.dk/2012-11-12-optaget-i-hall-fame-den-bedste-af-dem-alle |author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=12 November 2012|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Peter Schmeichel]]''' (1987–2001)<ref name="HoF-4">{{cite web|title=Schmeichel optaget i Hall of Fame|url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2009-11-09-schmeichel-optaget-i-hall-of-fame|publisher=TV2 Sport |author=Miriam Kjer |date=9 November 2009|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Thomas Helveg]]''' (1994–2007)<ref name="HoF-Hel">{{cite web |title=Fodboldens Hall of Fame indlemmer Thomas Helveg |url=https://www.dr.dk/sporten/seneste-sport/fodboldens-hall-fame-indlemmer-thomas-helveg |author=DR Sporten|date=10 June 2025|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Jon Dahl Tomasson]]''' (1997–2010)<ref name="HoF-Jon">{{cite web |title=Jon Dahl Tomasson hyldet inden landskamp |url=https://sport.tv2.dk/fodbold/2024-06-05-jon-dahl-tomasson-hyldet-inden-landskamp |author=((TV2 Sporten))|date=5 June 2024|language=da}}</ref>
#'''[[Morten Olsen]]''' (1970–1989, Manager 2000–2015)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bold.dk/fodbold/nyheder/morten-olsen-optaget-i-hall-of-fame/|title=Morten Olsen optaget i Hall of Fame|website=bold.dk|date=21 March 2016 }}</ref>
{{div col end}}

===Payment===
Each national team player receives a set amount of money per match, including bonuses for a win and qualification for European Championship and World Cup tournaments. Throughout the years, the prize money has gone from around €1,340 for a match win in 1987 and around €26,800 for the Euro 1988 participation alone, to around €67,000 for the 1998 World Cup, and up to €107,000 for the 2002 World Cup, per player.<ref>{{cite book|author=Heintze|first=Jan|title=Tyve år på toppen|publisher=Cicero|year=2002|isbn=87-7714-459-7|language=da}}</ref>

Currently, the payments for participation in games outside the big tournaments are regulated by a collective agreement&nbsp;– to be renegotiated after a certain number of years&nbsp;– while all payments for participation of players in a final tournament squad are regulated by the "1998 agreement" between the DBU and ''[[Spillerforeningen]]''. The "1998 agreement" stipulates that whenever the national team qualifies for a World Cup or European Championship tournament, the selected players for the final tournament shall receive 50% of the DBU's revenues from the event, and 65% of the DBU's revenues from the sale of merchandise and license agreements. Additionally, the sponsors of the national team also pay a relatively high "qualification bonus" each time the team qualifies for a final tournament. This bonus is shared between the group of players being selected for the final 18-man squad, to one or several of the qualification matches played, with the exact distribution normally decided according to the number of times the player was selected.<ref name="DBU payment">{{cite web|title=120 millioner kroner for VM-titel|url=http://www.dbu.dk/Nyheder/2010/Maj/120_millioner_kroner_for_VM-titel.aspx|author=DBU|date=3 June 2010|language=da}}</ref>

Each of the 23 selected players for the 2002 World Cup received DKK 498,000 (equal to €66,800) from the event revenues,<ref name="DBU payment" /> plus DKK 122,900 (equal to €16,500) from the sale of merchandise and license agreements, plus an unknown qualification bonus from the sponsors, plus the standard payment from the DBU each time they were selected for the final 18-man squad to a qualification match.<ref name="Player bonus">{{cite web|title=Player bonus|url=http://www.dbu.dk/oevrigt_indhold/Media/FIFA%202010%20Media%20Guide/Player%20bonus.aspx|author=DBU|date=10 June 2010|access-date=19 November 2010|archive-date=30 June 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100630024801/http://www.dbu.dk/oevrigt_indhold/Media/FIFA%202010%20Media%20Guide/Player%20bonus.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> In comparison, each of the 23 selected players for the 2010 World Cup received DKK 635,000 (equal to €85,100) from the event revenues, plus DKK 98,900 (equal to €13,200) from the sale of merchandise and license agreements, plus a qualification bonus from the sponsors at DKK 2,800,000 (equal to €375,000) to be shared between the players&nbsp;– corresponding to an amount of €2,080 per time the player was selected for the final 18-man squad to a qualification match. In addition to the qualification bonus, the players also received this standard payment from the DBU each time they were selected for the 18-man squad to a qualification match:<ref name="Player bonus" />

* '''Starting fee:''' home games = DKK 1,2 per spectator, away games = DKK 8,290
* '''Bonus for a draw:''' home games = DKK 0, away games = DKK 9,475
* '''Bonus for a win:''' home games = DKK 17,765, away games = DKK 29,600

Based on Denmark's results and number of spectators at home matches,<ref name="MO games" /> the standard qualification match payment to a player selected for all of Denmark's qualification matches equals a total amount of DKK 235,898 (or €31,600) for the six qualification matches in the 2008–09 season, and a total amount of DKK 170,788 (or €22,900) for the four qualification matches in the 2009–10 season. Finally, the players could also add a seasonal payment named "regular bonus" from the sponsors, with a total of DKK 4,350,000 (equal to €583,100) to be shared each season by the squad of 18 players selected for a match, meaning a player who was selected for all national team matches from 1 August to 31 July would receive an additional DKK 241,700 (equal to €32,400) payment in both the 2008–09 and 2009–10 seasons.<ref name="Player bonus" />

The last amount of a players income generated by the national team is the standard payment received for each friendly match:<ref name="Players bonus in 2004-2010">{{cite web|title=Players bonus (1st August 2004 to 31st July 2010)|url=http://diwa.sbu.dbu.dk/page.aspx?id=453|author=SBU|year=2004}}{{Dead link|date=February 2026 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref>
* '''Starting fee:''' home games = DKK 1,0 per spectator, away games = DKK 5,000
* '''Bonus for a draw:''' home games = DKK 0, away games = DKK 0
* '''Bonus for a win:''' home games = DKK 10,000, away games = DKK 10,000

Based on Denmark's results and number of spectators at home matches,<ref name="MO games" /> the standard payment to a player selected for all Denmark's friendly matches equals a total amount of DKK 41,426 (or €5,500) for the three friendly matches in the 2008–09 season, and a total amount of DKK 88,773 (or €11,900) for the seven friendly matches in the 2009–10 season.{{fact|date=December 2023}}

When all these payments are added together for a player, under the assumption a player was selected for all matches in each season as well as the final 2010 World Cup squad, he would have received a total payment from the DBU of €69,500 in the 2008–09 season and a total payment from the DBU of €186,300 in the 2009–10 season.{{fact|date=December 2023}}

==Player records==
{{See also|Denmark national football team records and statistics}}
{{updated|7 June 2026.}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Landsholdsspillere (50 + kampe) |url=https://www.dbu.dk/landshold/herrelandshold/a-landsholdet/landsholdsstatistik/landsholdsspillere-50-plus-kampe/ |publisher=Danish Football Association |language=da |access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Flest mål |url=https://www.dbu.dk/landshold/herrelandshold/a-landsholdet/landsholdsstatistik/flest-maal/ |publisher=Danish Football Association |language=da |access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref>
:''Players listed in '''bold''' are still active with Denmark.''

===Most appearances===

|-

! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!width="50"|Position
! style="width:100px;"|Period

|-

|-
| 3 || style="text-align:left" | [[Peter Schmeichel]] || 129 || 1 || GK || 1987–2001
|-
| 4 || style="text-align:left" | [[Dennis Rommedahl]] || 126 || 21 || MF || 2000–2013
|-
| 5 || style="text-align:left" | [[Kasper Schmeichel]] || 120 || 0 || GK || 2013–2025
|-
| 6 || style="text-align:left" | [[Jon Dahl Tomasson]] || 112 || 52 || FW || 1997–2010
|-
| 7 || style="text-align:left" | [[Thomas Helveg]] || 108 || 2 || DF || 1994–2007
|-
| 8 || style="text-align:left" | [[Michael Laudrup]] || 104 || 37 || MF/FW || 1982–1998
|-
| rowspan="2" | 9 || style="text-align:left" | [[Martin Jørgensen]] || 102 || 12 || MF || 1998–2011
|-
| style="text-align:left" | [[Morten Olsen]] || 102 || 4 || DF || 1970–1989
|-
|}

{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|Denmark's European Championship history
|-
!First match
|{{fb|DEN}} 0–3 {{fb-rt|URS}}<br />([[Barcelona]], Spain; 17 June 1964)
|-
!Biggest win
|{{fb|DEN}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|YUG}}<br />([[Lyon]], France; 16 June 1984)
|-
!Biggest defeat
|{{fb|DEN}} 0–3 {{fb-rt|URS}}<br />(Barcelona, Spain; 17 June 1964)<br>{{fb|CRO}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|DEN}}<br />([[Sheffield]], England; 16 June 1996)<br>{{fb|FRA|1974}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|DEN}}<br />([[Bruges]], Belgium; 11 June 2000)<br>{{fb|DEN}} 0–3 {{fb-rt|NED}}<br />([[Rotterdam]], Netherlands; 16 June 2000)<br>{{fb|CZE}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|DEN}}<br />([[Porto]], Portugal; 27 June 2004)
|-
!Best result
|'''Champions''' ([[UEFA Euro 1992|1992]])
|-
!Worst result
|Group stage ([[UEFA Euro 1988|1988]], [[UEFA Euro 1996|1996]], [[UEFA Euro 2000|2000]], [[UEFA Euro 2012|2012]])
|}

|-

| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|USA}} || 3 || 2 || 1 || 0 || 8 || 5 || +3
|-
|- style="background:#CCFFCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|URU}} || 2 || 2 || 0 || 0 || 8 || 2 || +6
|-
|- style="background:#CCFFCC"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|WAL}} || 11 || 7 || 0 || 4 || 16 || 9 || +7
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total || 927 || 436 || 191 || 300 || 1,684 || 1,235 || +479
|-
| colspan="8" | {{Notelist}}
|}

==Honours==
===Global===
* '''[[FIFA Confederations Cup]]'''
** {{gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[1995 King Fahd Cup|1995]]
* '''[[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]]'''
** {{silver2}} Silver medal (3): [[Football at the 1908 Summer Olympics|1908]], [[Football at the 1912 Summer Olympics|1912]], [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960]]
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal (1): [[Football at the 1948 Summer Olympics|1948]]

===Intercontinental===
* '''[[CONMEBOL–UEFA Cup of Champions]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[Artemio Franchi Trophy#1993|1993]]

===Continental===
* '''[[UEFA European Championship]]'''
** {{gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[UEFA Euro 1992|1992]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Nordic Football Championship]]'''
** '''Champions (3)''': [[1924–28 Nordic Football Championship|1924–28]], [[1978–80 Nordic Football Championship|1978–80]], [[1981–85 Nordic Football Championship|1981–85]]
** Runners-up (7): [[1933–36 Nordic Football Championship|1933–36]], [[1937–47 Nordic Football Championship|1937–47]], [[1948–51 Nordic Football Championship|1948–51]], [[1960–63 Nordic Football Championship|1960–63]], [[1964–67 Nordic Football Championship|1964–67]], [[1968–71 Nordic Football Championship|1968–71]], [[1972–77 Nordic Football Championship|1972–77]]
** Third place (4): [[1929–32 Nordic Football Championship|1929–32]], [[1952–55 Nordic Football Championship|1952–55]], [[1956–59 Nordic Football Championship|1956–59]], [[2000–01 Nordic Football Championship|2000–01]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[Lunar New Year Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (2)''': 1994, 2006
* '''[[King's Cup (Thailand)|King's Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (1)''': [[2010 King's Cup|2010]]

===Awards===
* '''[[FIFA Fair Play Award]]''': [[The Best FIFA Football Awards 2021#FIFA Fair Play Award|2021]]
* '''[[World Soccer (magazine)#Men's World Team of the Year|World Soccer Team of the Year]]''': 1992
* '''[[Guerin Sportivo#Team of the Year|Guerin Sportivo Team of the Year]]''': 1983
* '''[[UNESCO]] Fair Play Trophy''': [[2002 Lunar New Year Cup|2002]]<ref>{{cite news | url = https://www.panathlon-international.org/images/home/quaderni/fair_play/Fair%20Play%20INGLESE.pdf | title= UNESCO Fair Play 1964-2005 | publisher = Panathlon International }}</ref>

===Summary===

|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total

|0||3||1||4
|-

|1||0||0||1
|-
|align=left|[[UEFA European Championship]]
|1||0||0||1
|-
| align="left" |[[CONMEBOL–UEFA Cup of Champions]]
|0||1||0||1

!Total!!2!!4!!1!!7
|}
